sourcePaper.title: Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and the fate of double-stranded breaks
sourcePaper.sourcePdf: workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf

boundedMetadata.studySystem: mouse spermatogenic cells during meiotic prophase
boundedMetadata.mainClaimHint: PRDM9-associated H3K4me3 at recombination hotspots is linked to double-strand break timing, chromatin openness, and crossover-biased repair outcomes.
boundedMetadata.copyrightBoundary: Only title-level bibliographic metadata and short paraphrased claim hints are stored; no abstract or body text is reproduced.

minimumInputs: stage-resolved H3K4me3 peak calls or signal tracks
PRDM9 binding or PRDM9-dependent hotspot annotations
crossover and noncrossover proxy annotations when available
planChecklist: Record available raw and processed data accessions before analysis.
Normalize stage-specific H3K4me3 signal and call comparable hotspot/domain sets.
Measure overlap among PRDM9-associated regions, H3K4me3 peaks, and DSB proxy hotspots.
Compare chromatin accessibility between early and late DSB proxy groups.
Estimate association between DSB timing proxies and crossover-biased outcome proxies.
Emit structured partial or failed status when a required data class is missing.
sensitivityChecks: Vary peak caller and signal thresholds for H3K4me3 hotspot calls.
Repeat overlap analysis by replicate and by meiotic stage.
Control for stage composition and hotspot strength as confounders.
Test whether accessibility association persists after excluding extreme signal regions.
negativeResultChecks: If H3K4me3 hotspot strength does not predict DSB proxies after stage controls, downgrade mechanistic support.
If accessibility signal is explained by stage or assay batch, report a confounded association.
If crossover proxy enrichment disappears under threshold changes, mark the claim as unstable.
rubricPrompts: Separate correlation, temporal ordering, perturbation, and direct mechanism evidence.
Require explicit missing-data notes for unavailable CO/NCO proxy inputs.
Allow a negative scientific conclusion without treating it as execution failure.
